title: [phase:critique] [family:prototype-slice] [provider_hint:codex] Challenge this prototype slice: implement a local Python tmux wrapper with allow operations (has-session, list-sessions, list-windows, capture-pane), gated operations (select-window, select-pane, new-session, kill-session, rename-sessio...
summary_text: 1. `--approve` is too coarse and likely bypassable. Require per-operation approval tokens (scoped to op + target + TTL), and never let one approval authorize multiple gated/destructive actions like `kill-session` or `paste-text`. 2. `paste-text`/`submit` are a command-injection surface. You need hard guards on control bytes/escape sequences, max payload length, explicit target pane, and an optional “no-enter” mode so `submit` cannot be triggered implicitly by pasted content. 3. There is a TOCTOU race between check and act. `has-session`/`list-*` results can go stale before `select-*`/`kill-*`; bind actions to stable identifiers (pane/window IDs), lock execution per tmux socket, and re-validate target identity immediately before execution. 4. JSONL audit logging is incomplete unless tamper-resistant and full-fidelity. Log request + resolved tmux argv + actor + target IDs + decision path + exit status + stderr, then fsync append; add chaining/signatures or immutable sink to detect deletion/rewrite. 5. Macros are fixed in name, but not necessarily fixed in effect. `continue-session`/`launch-claude-code` must pin absolute binary paths, sanitize env/PATH, enforce timeouts/resource limits, and support `--dry-run` that emits exactly the final quoted command sequence to prevent hidden behavior drift.
